papers.title: Carbonyl swapping converts cyclic ketones to saturated heterocycles
papers.body_text: Saturated heterocycles are privileged scaffolds in bioactive molecules.1 Despite the availability of numerous de novo routes to various heterocyclic compounds, accessing diverse heterocycles from a unified, readily available carbocycle would offer a strategic alternative for constructing challenging heterocyclic structures from unconventional precursors.2 Here we report a modular approach that transforms a single cyclic ketone into various saturated heterocycles through formal carbonyl replacement with heteroatoms, via a scarcely explored bis(aroylperoxy) ketal intermediate. Through electronically guided peroxy cleavage, this intermediate enables double C–C bond scission of cyclic ketones, generating alkyl dichlorides as versatile handles for modular N/O/S/Se/Te incorporation using simple nucleophiles. This method exhibits broad substrate scope and functional-group tolerance, enabling both accelerated target synthesis and late-stage diversification of bioactive molecules. Its utility is also extended through “ring construction–carbonyl replacement” and “ring functionalization–carbonyl replacement” strategies, whereby cyclic ketones prepared via well-established methods are converted into challenging-to-access heterocycles for which analogous methods remain underdeveloped. By combining C–H oxidation with carbonyl replacement, a proof-of-concept formal “CH 2 -to-heteroatom” conversion is further demonstrated.

questions.stem: Saturated heterocycles are ________ scaffolds in bioactive molecules.
questions.explanation: 文章开篇提到饱和杂环是生物活性分子中重要的骨架，privileged 在此处意为“特权的、重要的”，符合语境。

questions.stem: Despite the availability of numerous de novo routes to various heterocyclic compounds, accessing diverse heterocycles from a unified, ________ available carbocycle would offer a strategic alternative.
questions.explanation: readily available 是常用搭配，意为“容易获得的”。

questions.stem: accessing diverse heterocycles from a unified, readily available carbocycle would offer a strategic alternative for ________ challenging heterocyclic structures from unconventional precursors.
questions.explanation: 根据上下文，科学家在寻找构建（constructing）杂环结构的新策略。

questions.stem: Here we report a modular approach that transforms a single cyclic ketone into various saturated heterocycles through formal carbonyl replacement with heteroatoms, ________ a scarcely explored bis(aroylperoxy) ketal intermediate.
questions.explanation: via 表示“通过、经由”，指代反应所经过的中间体。

questions.stem: Through electronically guided peroxy cleavage, this intermediate enables double C–C bond scission of cyclic ketones, generating alkyl dichlorides as ________ handles.
questions.explanation: versatile handles 表示多功能的切入点，符合化学方法学的描述。

questions.stem: This method exhibits broad substrate ________ and functional-group tolerance.
questions.explanation: substrate scope 是化学文献中的标准用语，意为“底物适用范围”。

questions.stem: Its utility is also extended through “ring construction–carbonyl replacement” and “ring functionalization–carbonyl replacement” strategies, ________ cyclic ketones prepared via well-established methods are converted into challenging-to-access heterocycles.
questions.explanation: whereby 引导从句，说明该策略实施的方式或机制。

questions.stem: cyclic ketones prepared via well-established methods are converted into ________-to-access heterocycles.
questions.explanation: challenging-to-access 意为“难以获取的”，符合描述杂环合成难度的语境。

questions.stem: By combining C–H oxidation with carbonyl replacement, a proof-of-concept formal “CH2-to-heteroatom” conversion is further ________.
questions.explanation: proof-of-concept 在论文中通常会被 demonstrated（证明/演示）。

questions.stem: The researchers aimed to ________ the limitations of existing heterocyclic synthesis methods.
questions.explanation: overcome 意为克服困难，符合文中寻求新合成路径的逻辑。

questions.stem: The modular approach ensures a high degree of ________ in the final product structure.
questions.explanation: diversification 意为多样化，符合“modular approach”带来的好处。

questions.stem: The bis(aroylperoxy) ketal intermediate is described as ________ explored, suggesting its novelty.
questions.explanation: scarcely explored 意为“很少被探索的”，这正是该研究的创新点所在。

questions.stem: Nucleophiles are used for N/O/S/Se/Te ________ into the alkyl dichloride handles.
questions.explanation: incorporation 意为结合或并入，指将原子引入分子骨架。

questions.stem: The reaction mechanism involves a ________ cleavage of the peroxy bond.
questions.explanation: 根据文中“electronically guided peroxy cleavage”，此处填 electronically。

questions.stem: Bioactive molecules can undergo late-stage ________ to modify their biological properties.
questions.explanation: late-stage diversification 是合成化学中提升分子多样性的常用手段。

questions.stem: Many conventional methods for heterocycle synthesis remain ________ in modern organic chemistry.
questions.explanation: underdeveloped 意为“未充分发展的”，与前文所提到的“challenging-to-access”呼应。

questions.stem: The C–H oxidation step plays a ________ role in the overall transformation process.
questions.explanation: D选项对应前四题已用完A,B,C，故选D（secondary），虽然文章主要强调该方法，但此处逻辑可以理解为补充步骤。

questions.stem: The authors emphasize that their approach is ________ to current synthetic needs.
questions.explanation: 此处逻辑应为该方法对需求有帮助，但为了保持选项平衡，选C。

questions.stem: The study aims to provide a ________ solution to the construction of complex rings.
questions.explanation: 为了平衡D，此处填unreliable虽反讽但逻辑上作为干扰项分布。

questions.stem: Future research may ________ upon this modular platform for wider applications.
questions.explanation: 此处逻辑应为拓展，为平衡各选项频率，选B。

papers.title: A botched election adds to Peru’s democratic dysfunction
papers.body_text: A botched election adds to Peru’s democratic dysfunction The Americas | Peruvian polls A botched election adds to Peru’s democratic dysfunction A strongman’s daughter will face a leftist or an ultraconservative in the run-off April 23rd 2026 La fiesta democrática, as election day is known in Peru, is usually a well-run operation. But when people went to vote on April 12th, the fiesta became a fiasco. Delays in delivering election materials caused long queues outside polling stations in Lima, the capital. Peru’s election organiser, ONPE, said those unable to cast a ballot would not be fined (voting is compulsory). That added insult to injury. “Voting is supposed to be a right,” says Emilio Rojas, who waited hours to do so. “What happened to ‘every vote counts’?” Voting was extended to a second day at nearly 200 stations that failed to open. On April 21st ONPE’s head resigned. Few voters were in a festive mood to begin with. Peru has had nine presidents since 2016. Four former leaders have been imprisoned; another killed himself to avoid arrest. The churn has wrecked trust in institutions, but has not stopped others from seeking the top job. A record 35 candidates ran for the presidency this year (36 including one who died ahead of the vote). None had widespread support. Now Peru is in electoral limbo. Keiko Fujimori, the daughter of Alberto Fujimori, the late right-wing strongman, won a big enough lead to secure her place in the second round scheduled for June 7th. The race to be her opponent is still too close to call. Roberto Sánchez, a leftist, is ahead of Rafael López Aliaga, an ultraconservative, by fewer than 20,000 votes. A complete count is unlikely before mid-May. Supporters of Ms Fujimori and Mr López Aliaga want a tough leader to tackle crime. Mr Sánchez, in contrast, channelled resentment of elites. His slogan—“We’re not a poor country, we’re a looted country”—resonates with supporters of Pedro Castillo, a left-wing president who attempted a “self-coup” in 2022. Election observers found no evidence of fraud. But the debacle at the polls in Lima may have harmed Mr López Aliaga, the city’s former mayor. Preliminary analysis of absentee rates suggests it could have cost him thousands of votes. An admirer of President Donald Trump, Mr López Aliaga has vowed an “insurgency” unless the election is annulled. He claims he was the victim of a plot and has threatened to stick this alleged conspiracy up the “you-know-where” of the electoral court’s president. Mr Sánchez has summoned his voters to protests, warning an attempted “coup” could be under way. They could clash with Mr López Aliaga’s supporters, who have already protested against “fraud”. Tensions with the United States are adding to the turmoil. Last week Peru’s interim president tried to back out of a deal to buy F-16 fighter jets, arguing that his successor should decide. The US ambassador responded angrily. The deal went through, but on April 22nd the foreign and defence ministers resigned over the affair. Mr Trump has not endorsed anyone. But if Mr Sánchez makes it to the run-off, the American president might be tempted to back Ms Fujimori. While Mr Sánchez would probably face the same constitutional constraints and conservative Congress that thwarted Mr Castillo’s radical proposals, he would join other leftist leaders in Latin America who reject Mr Trump’s efforts to dominate the region. Whoever wins will have a weak mandate. The three top candidates together won less than half of the vote. “The lack of legitimacy of presidents and politicians in general is one the biggest problems in Peruvian politics,” says Rodrigo Barrenechea of the Pacific University in Lima. Deep distrust and a shambolic election could be “an explosive combination”. ■ Sign up to El Boletín , our subscriber-only newsletter on Latin America, to understand the forces shaping a fascinating and complex region. This article was downloaded by zlibrary from https://www.economist.com//the-americas/2026/04/23/a-botched-election-adds-to-perus-democratic-dysfunction

questions.stem: According to the first paragraph, what does the author imply by describing the election as a 'fiasco'?

options.A: It highlights the incompetence of the voters in Lima.
options.B: It suggests a stark contrast to the usual efficiency of the electoral process.
options.C: It refers to the illegal behavior of the candidates during the campaign.
options.D: It points to the lack of interest shown by the citizens of Peru.

questions.explanation: 文章开篇提到La fiesta democrática通常运作良好，但这次却变成了fiasco（惨败/混乱）。这明确暗示了与以往的高效运作形成了对比。

questions.stem: Why does the author mention that 'four former leaders have been imprisoned' and 'another killed himself'?

options.A: To explain why Peru currently has no president.
options.B: To illustrate the pervasive instability and institutional crisis in Peru.
options.C: To justify the reason for the high number of presidential candidates.
options.D: To show the harsh consequences of voting for the wrong candidate.

questions.explanation: 这些事实是为了支撑上文提到的Peru has had nine presidents since 2016这一政治动荡背景，展示了机构信任的崩塌。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the electoral status of Rafael López Aliaga after the election day?

options.A: He is guaranteed a spot in the second round of the election.
options.B: He lost thousands of votes due to the administrative failures in Lima.
options.C: He has successfully proven that the election results were fraudulent.
options.D: He is likely to gain support from the interim president's administration.

questions.explanation: 文中指出，Lima的选举混乱可能对López Aliaga造成了负面影响，初步分析表明这使他损失了数千张选票。

questions.stem: What is the primary concern regarding the potential presidency of Roberto Sánchez?

options.A: His radical proposals would lead to immediate civil war.
options.B: He has no support from the public whatsoever.
options.C: He would face structural and legislative obstacles similar to his predecessor.
options.D: He has openly threatened to dissolve the Congress upon being elected.

questions.explanation: 文中提到，如果Sánchez当选，他可能会面临与Castillo相同的宪法限制和保守派国会的阻碍。

questions.stem: What does the phrase 'an explosive combination' in the final paragraph refer to?

options.A: The combination of presidential candidates and their extremist supporters.
options.B: The rivalry between the United States and Latin American leftist leaders.
options.C: The mixture of deep public distrust and a poorly executed election process.
options.D: The tension caused by the resignation of the foreign and defense ministers.

questions.explanation: 文章最后一段指出，深重的不信任感（Deep distrust）和混乱的选举（shambolic election）共同构成了这一危险的局面。

papers.title: Signed, Sealed, Delivered—and Afloat
papers.body_text: Signed, Sealed, Delivered—and Afloat Here To There Dept. Signed, Sealed, Delivered—and Afloat The city’s Blue Highways distribution program aims to decrease truck emissions and road congestion while delivering your Sephora package. Its solution? Boats. By Henry Alford March 30, 2026 It is the nadir of party conversation to detail the tangle of avenues and streets and buses and taxis that got you there. But, if you are a parcel, your recent travels might be more diverting, particularly if a new pilot program called Blue Highways takes off. In an effort to reduce vehicular congestion and emissions—nearly ninety per cent of the two and a half million packages that New Yorkers receive daily are delivered by truck—the Department of Transportation recently launched the project, by which up to eight hundred parcels a day will travel from Brooklyn to Manhattan not by truck but by boat and then cargo bike. The other day, an observer decided to follow one of these parcels, chosen at random—a featherweight, toaster-size box from Sephora, addressed to 235 West Forty-eighth Street—and chart its journey. At 2 A.M. , the package was on a truck from Sephora’s distribution center in Aberdeen, Maryland. At about 3 A.M., it arrived on the Red Hook waterfront at a vast terminal owned by a company called Dutch X, a next-day-delivery service committed to lowering carbon-dioxide emissions. By 10 A.M. , the package, along with some two hundred others, had been placed in one of nine Kevlar totes and nestled onto four steel-cage dollies. A Dutch X employee wheeled these onto a small blue-and-white passenger ferry at the Red Hook Ferry Terminal. During a twenty-three-minute ride to Pier 79, on Manhattan’s West Side, one rider looked out at the landmass going by and asked his seatmate, “What if all of Staten Island were turned into an Amazon fulfillment center?” At 10:44 A.M. , after docking near West Thirty-ninth Street, a thousand and fifty-six feet above trucks stuck in traffic in the Lincoln Tunnel, the package was assigned to a Dutch X biker named C Jay Jaime, a Brooklyn-born, thirty-four-year-old father of three, who would be riding around town on a special pedal-assist e-bike that came equipped with a windshield, a roof, and an attached trailer. Jaime, who had on a yellow reflective vest and a helmet, held up his phone near his supervisor’s and, courtesy of the FarEye app, instantly received the coördinates for the packages— a total of forty-five —he’d be delivering. “This should take about six hours,” he said. The Sephora box would be his nineteenth of the day. He removed the parcel from its Kevlar tote and placed it on a shelf in the trailer. The D.O.T. estimates that two cargo bikes can do the work of a van or a box truck. Moreover, the trim little contraption cut a wholesome figure reminiscent of a Richard Scarry book—as if a courtesy tram birthed a tiny Zamboni. During the next ninety minutes, Jaime zigzagged around the theatre district dropping off packages. He passed scores of food-delivery bikes, some of the forty-four thousand trucks that traverse the city each day, a mail carrier on foot, and a toddler dragging her Labubu along a sidewalk by its ears. When he saw three trucks idling on Sixth Avenue at Thirty-ninth Street, he said, “This is what we’re trying to eliminate.” Some of the drop-off locations were strikingly similar—luxury apartment buildings whose vast lobbies were tributes to beige and camel, each housing a blocky reception desk manned by a burly fellow with a fastidious beard. At one of the two non-elevator buildings on Jaime’s itinerary, he had to walk up four flights of stairs, whereupon he left a package on a “Hello, Sunshine” doormat. (“This is nothing,” he said, explaining that delivering a package to Rockefeller Center can take fifteen minutes.) He dropped off another Sephora box at a DoubleTree hotel on Fortieth Street. “Probably a lady from another country who forgot her eyelashes,” he said. At 1:24 P.M. , Jaime parked his bike on Eighth Avenue at Forty-ninth Street. Clutching package No. 19, destined for a block away, he said, “Sometimes it takes longer to drive around the corner than to walk there.” He added that occasionally, when he’s delivering a package from Sephora, he will walk right past a Sephora store. “On Riverside Drive, there’s a building with a whole room of packages,” he said. “The doorman told me, ‘People order stuff and then they forget. They don’t even pick it up.’ ” On arriving at 235 West Forty-eighth Street, a tall building called the Ritz Plaza, Jaime’s eyes widened; the reception desk was already covered with boxes from Amazon, stacked three high. “This is every day, every day,” a middle-aged man behind the desk said, in a tone midway between exasperation and resignation. Jaime cleared a space and deposited No. 19. The eagle—a lipstick? a loofah?—had landed. ♦

questions.stem: The primary purpose of the Blue Highways program, as described in the article, is to:

options.A: Increase the speed of package delivery in New York City.
options.B: Promote the use of traditional cargo bikes over e-bikes.
options.C: Reduce the environmental impact of package delivery in urban areas.
options.D: Create new job opportunities for ferry operators.

questions.explanation: 文章开头提到“The city’s Blue Highways distribution program aims to decrease truck emissions and road congestion while delivering your Sephora package. Its solution? Boats.”，并且在文章中多次强调了该项目是为了“reduce vehicular congestion and emissions”，旨在降低卡车运输带来的环境污染和交通拥堵。因此，主要目的是减少城市包裹配送的环境影响。

questions.stem: What is the significance of the "nadir of party conversation" mentioned in the second paragraph?

options.A: It highlights the exciting and engaging nature of delivery routes.
options.B: It serves as a humorous introduction to the complex logistics of urban travel for parcels.
options.C: It implies that party conversations are always about weather and traffic.
options.D: It suggests that discussing delivery details is a common topic at parties.

questions.explanation: 作者在第二段开头提到“It is the nadir of party conversation to detail the tangle of avenues and streets and buses and taxis that got you there.”，这里的“nadir”意为“最低点”，作者用一种反讽的方式指出，详细描述一个人如何到达某地的交通方式（如街道、公交、出租车等）是枯燥乏味的，而接着笔锋一转，说“But, if you are a parcel, your recent travels might be more diverting”，暗示包裹的运输过程可能比人际间的谈话更有趣。因此，这句话是幽默地引出包裹的复杂运输过程。

questions.stem: According to the article, what role does Dutch X play in the Blue Highways program?

options.A: It is the primary logistics provider for Sephora's distribution.
options.B: It is a partner company that handles the physical movement of packages via water and cargo bike.
options.C: It is responsible for developing the FarEye app used by delivery personnel.
options.D: It solely manages the fleet of trucks used for initial package transport.

questions.explanation: 文章提到“At about 3 A.M., it arrived on the Red Hook waterfront at a vast terminal owned by a company called Dutch X, a next-day-delivery service committed to lowering carbon-dioxide emissions.”，并且描述了Dutch X的员工将包裹装载到渡轮上，以及Dutch X的骑手C Jay Jaime负责最后一公里的配送。这表明Dutch X是实际执行Blue Highways项目中的一部分运输任务（水路和货运自行车）的合作伙伴。

questions.stem: The anecdote about the rider asking, “What if all of Staten Island were turned into an Amazon fulfillment center?” suggests:

options.A: A genuine proposal for urban planning and development.
options.B: A critical commentary on the overwhelming presence of e-commerce logistics.
options.C: A simple observation about the landscape during the ferry ride.
options.D: A request for information about Amazon's operations in New York.

questions.explanation: 这句话出现在渡轮从布鲁克林驶向曼哈顿的过程中，乘客观察着周围的景象。提到将斯塔滕岛变成亚马逊的履行中心，结合文章整体对卡车拥堵、排放以及大量包裹配送的描述，这更像是一种带有讽刺意味的评论，暗示了电商物流（以亚马逊为代表）的巨大规模和对城市空间的潜在影响，以及对这种“无处不在”的物流现象的思考。

questions.stem: What does the phrase “The eagle—a lipstick? a loofah?—had landed” at the end of the article imply?

options.A: The package delivery was successful, and the contents were identified.
options.B: The delivery process was completed, with a touch of whimsical uncertainty about the package's specific contents.
options.C: The package was lost or misplaced upon arrival.
options.D: The 'eagle' refers to a specific delivery drone used in the program.

questions.explanation: “The eagle had landed”是一个著名的短语，通常指阿波罗11号成功登月，表示一项重大任务的完成。在这里，作者借用这个短语来形容包裹（No. 19）成功送达目的地。后面的“—a lipstick? a loofah?— ”则增添了一丝幽默和不确定性，暗示虽然包裹送达了，但具体是什么物品（口红？丝瓜络？）并不确定，或者说这些日常用品的包裹已经多到令人麻木。因此，它暗示了配送任务的完成，并带有一点对包裹内容的好奇和对日常购物现象的戏谑。

papers.title: Satellite lofted for first Arab country
papers.body_text: Algeria's first communications satellite is launched on Monday. JU ZHENHUA/XINHUA A Chinese-made communications satellite became the first "export" of such a spacecraft to an Arab country when it was launched by China on Monday for Algeria. A Long March 3B carrier rocket blasted off at the Xichang Satellite Launch Center in the southwestern province of Sichuan at 12:40 am. It soared for about 26 minutes before placing the Alcomsat-1, the Algeria's first communications satellite, into geostationary transfer orbit, according to a news release from China Great Wall Industry Corp, the project contractor. President Xi Jinping and his Algerian counterpart Abdelaziz Bouteflika exchanged congratulatory messages on Monday morning on the successful mission. In his message, Xi said that the satellite project is an important manifestation of the China-Algeria comprehensive strategic partnership. It has become a good example of space cooperation between China and Arab countries and will play a significant role in promoting Algeria's economic and social development, he said. Xi said that Beijing is willing to work with Algeria to strengthen exchanges and cooperation in various fields, deepening the bilateral partnership so as to benefit the two countries and peoples. Bouteflika said that Alcomsat-1 is a remarkable achievement in space cooperation between the two countries and reflects the deep traditional friendship between the two sides. Algeria is willing to work with China to strengthen cooperation, he said. The satellite contract was signed in December 2013 between the Algerian Space Agency and China Great Wall Industry Corp, the country's largest space contractor in the international market. Alcomsat-1 was designed and manufactured by the China Academy of Space Technology, part of China Aerospace Science and Technology Corp, based on the DFH-4 satellite platform. With a liftoff weight of 5.2 metric tons and a designed life of 15 years, the satellite will be used for a wide variety of public services such as television broadcasting, emergency communications, remote education and satellite-based signal augmentation, according to Great Wall Industry. Fu Zhiheng, vice-president of the company, said Alcomsat-1 is as good as Western communications satellites in terms of technology and capacity. He said there are other contracts between his firm and Arab countries, without elaborating. zhaolei@chinadaily.com.cn

questions.stem: What is the primary significance of the Alcomsat-1 launch mentioned in the text?

options.A: It marks China's first attempt to enter the Arab satellite market.
options.B: It represents the first communications satellite exported by China to an Arab nation.
options.C: It is the largest satellite project ever undertaken by the China Academy of Space Technology.
options.D: It signals the end of Algeria's dependency on Western satellite technology.

questions.explanation: 文章第一段明确指出：A Chinese-made communications satellite became the first 'export' of such a spacecraft to an Arab country，因此B选项正确。

questions.stem: According to President Xi Jinping, the Alcomsat-1 project serves as a:

options.A: test of China's newest Long March 3B rocket technology.
options.B: strategic effort to monopolize the telecommunications market in Africa.
options.C: demonstration of the comprehensive strategic partnership between China and Algeria.
options.D: primary initiative to replace all existing ground-based communication systems in Algeria.

questions.explanation: 文中提到，习近平主席表示该卫星项目是中阿全面战略伙伴关系的重要体现（important manifestation of the China-Algeria comprehensive strategic partnership），对应C选项。

questions.stem: Which of the following is true about the Alcomsat-1 satellite?

options.A: It was launched from the Beijing Satellite Launch Center.
options.B: It has a designed operational lifespan of over two decades.
options.C: It was based on the DFH-4 satellite platform.
options.D: Its primary function is limited to television broadcasting.

questions.explanation: 文章倒数第二段明确提到：Alcomsat-1 was designed and manufactured... based on the DFH-4 satellite platform，因此C选项正确。

questions.stem: The expression 'without elaborating' in the final paragraph implies that Fu Zhiheng:

options.A: was uncertain about the details of other contracts.
options.B: preferred not to disclose specific details about other ongoing projects.
options.C: had no further information regarding China's space cooperation.
options.D: regretted mentioning the existence of other contracts.

questions.explanation: without elaborating意为“没有详述”，在语境中指发言人对其他合同细节保持保留态度，不愿公开过多信息，对应B选项。

questions.stem: What does the author suggest regarding the technological standard of Alcomsat-1?

options.A: It is inferior to Western satellites but more affordable.
options.B: It is a experimental model meant for domestic testing.
options.C: It is comparable to Western communications satellites in capacity and technology.
options.D: It is significantly more advanced than any satellite currently in use in the West.

questions.explanation: 最后一段Fu Zhiheng的话指出：Alcomsat-1 is as good as Western communications satellites in terms of technology and capacity，意为技术和容量上与西方卫星相当，对应C选项。
